content:
Every Angular Project Starts Clean

3 min read
12 hours ago

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

When an Angular project is new, everything feels organized.
You create features.
You separate folders.
You build components.
Your architecture looks healthy.
Then one day someone writes:
import { CustomerService } from '../customers/customer.service';It solves today’s problem.
Nobody worries.
After all…
It’s just one import.
One Import Never Stays Alone
Weeks later another feature needs customer data.
Another import appears.
Then another.
Soon your application looks like this:
Customers ─────► Orders
▲ │
│ ▼
Reports ◄──── Notifications
▲ │
└──── PaymentsEvery feature imports something from another feature.
The application still works.
